channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: An Excerpt from Chandrani Ghosh’s ‘Heartlines: A Love Triangle’
item.description: This excerpt from Chandrani Ghosh’s novel *Heartlines: A Love Triangle* dives into the emotional turmoil of Sharmila Basu, a woman who appears to have everything in order yet faces an unexpected emoti...

item.title: Practical Guide for New Parents: When a Bottle Warmer Makes Sense and When It Doesn't
item.description: When one new mama or papa dey start that parenting journey, dem go quickly understand say feeding baby sometimes no too easy – especially when baby dey cry for midnight hunger. This article from Pulse...

item.title: Yenagoa Set to Host 2025 Governor Douye Diri National Wrestling Classics After Reschedule
item.description: Yenagoa, wey be the capital of Bayelsa State, don ready to host the 2025 Governor Douye Diri National Wrestling Classics from March 25 go reach April 1, 2026. The competition bin suppose happen for De...

item.title: Driver Pursues Thieves After Watch Stolen in Mayfair
item.description: A dramatic robbery occurred in Mayfair when Besart Krasniqi, a driver, was targeted by three men while taking a photo of his Bentley outside a café. The thieves, two of whom were Algerian migrants, sn...

item.title: Rita Panahi spotted at Melbourne bistro while media discusses Trump and gambling risks
item.description: Columnist Rita Panahi was recently seen dining at Melbourne’s French bistro France-Soir, enjoying escargot while keeping her attention on her phone. The report, from a tipster, paints a humorous image...

item.title: Pakistan emphasizes diplomacy amid reports of potential US-Iran talks in Islamabad
item.description: On Tuesday, the Foreign Office (FO) advised media outlets to avoid speculation regarding Islamabad possibly hosting talks between the United States and Iranian officials. FO spokesperson Tahir Andrabi...

item.title: BTS World Tour’s $5.3 Billion Economic Impact, With Toronto Stops on the Horizon
item.description: The global economic phenomenon surrounding BTS's world tour is estimated to generate a staggering $5.32 billion in revenue across the 44 cities the K-pop supergroup will visit. Fans, known as the ‘ARM...

item.title: TV expert Mel Schilling dies aged 54 after brave battle with cancer
item.description: Television personality and relationship expert Mel Schilling, best known for her role on Married at First Sight (MAFS), has died aged 54 following a two-year battle with cancer. Her husband, Gareth Sc...

item.title: Supreme Court Questions Mamata Banerjee on Rights of ED Officers During Duty
item.description: The Supreme Court of India recently addressed a question of significant importance regarding the rights of Enforcement Directorate (ED) officers while performing their official duties. This came amid ...

item.title: Stellar Rotation Enhances Mixing in Red Giant Stars, Solving Longstanding Surface Chemistry Mystery
item.description: Astronomers have resolved a decades-old puzzle about red giant stars: how material from their nuclear-burning cores reaches the surface, altering the star's chemical composition. For nearly 50 years, ...

item.title: UN Contributes Sh 59.5 Billion to Kenya's Development Initiatives in 2025
item.description: In 2025, the United Nations invested around Sh 59.5 billion (approximately $460 million) in various development programmes across Kenya, according to the latest annual results report presented to the ...

item.title: Khan Academy Philippines Aims to Broaden Access to Quality Education
item.description: Khan Academy Philippines, a local licensee of the global non-profit, is working to address the ongoing education crisis in the Philippines by providing free, world-class educational resources. The ini...

item.title: DepEd increases school feeding program budget to P25.6B to support 4.6M learners
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) has announced a historic allocation of P25.6 billion for its School-Based Feeding Program (SBFP) for the 2026–2027 school year. This budget is more than double last...

item.title: Africa seeks fair global trade terms through reform in agriculture, digital trade, and green industry at WTO 2026
item.description: African countries continue to face major hurdles in global trade, with wealthy nations subsidising their farmers and skewing the market. These subsidies make it harder for African producers to compete...

item.title: NASA Releases High-Resolution Images Revealing Detailed Views of Solar System Planets
item.description: NASA has shared a stunning collection of high-resolution images that provide some of the clearest and most detailed views yet of the planets in our solar system. What were once seen as mere distant po...

item.title: Delhi High Court dismisses Lalu Prasad Yadav’s plea to quash CBI charges in land-for-jobs case
item.description: The Delhi High Court has rejected a petition by Rashtriya Janata Dal (RJD) chief and former Bihar Chief Minister Lalu Prasad Yadav, who sought to quash a Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) case aga...

item.title: Crisis in Côte d'Ivoire as Unsold Cocoa Piles Up, Farmers Struggle with Losses
item.description: In Côte d'Ivoire, the world’s top cocoa producer, frustration is growing among cocoa farmers as unsold stocks of cocoa beans continue to pile up in warehouses. The country’s cocoa sector, which is cru...

item.title: Kim Jong-un reinforces commitment to North Korea's nuclear status
item.description: In a defiant statement, North Korean leader Kim Jong-un has pledged to solidify his country's nuclear capabilities, calling the move irreversible. This declaration comes amidst growing tensions with t...

item.title: Renaldo Leaner Talks About His Call-Up to the Bafana Bafana Squad
item.description: Sekhukhune United goalkeeper Renaldo Leaner expressed that he wasn’t taken aback by his inclusion in the Bafana Bafana squad for their upcoming friendlies against Panama. The first of the two matches ...

item.title: Tiger Woods Eyes Potential Return to Competitive Golf at The Masters 2026
item.description: Tiger Woods, the five-time Masters champion, has not ruled out a return to competitive golf at Augusta National, with the 2026 tournament set to begin on 9 April. Having last competed professionally a...
